description: Lock Darwin's natural-selection node to the 1859 Origin scope and its source-backed geology, comparative anatomy, and exploration evidence edges.

checks.reason: The node is anchored to the 1859 first edition of Darwin's On the Origin of Species.
Origin uses geological relations, geological record limits, and geological succession as evidence for descent over time.
Origin explicitly relies on mutual affinities, morphology, embryology, and rudimentary organs, matching comparative anatomy rather than generic human anatomy.
Darwin ties the origin problem to H.M.S. Beagle observations and treats geographical distribution as a major evidentiary line.
The Darwin node should not depend directly on broad human anatomy when the source-backed evidence is comparative anatomy and morphology across organisms.
